title: carex vulgaris var. gaudichaudiana
intro: Carex vulgaris var. gaudichaudiana, also known as the rough sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Carex vulgaris var. gaudichaudiana include its potential to aid in the treatment of kidney stones and urinary tract infections due to its diuretic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for these purposes.
